reasoning: A well-constructed timing-based blind CBC padding oracle with multiple defensive layers: IV XOR masking, proof-of-work rate limiting, HMAC token alignment, and a timing side-channel (80ms delta, 5ms jitter) instead of distinct error messages. The PoW and HMAC are individually weak (trivial computation, attacker-known inputs) but collectively force the solver to understand the full protocol before automating the oracle. Flag isolation is strong (build-time encryption, file deletion, privilege drop, init script removal). The unused crypto_utils.py is dead code and the intended-solution description references error-based differentiation that contradicts the actual timing-oracle implementation, but overall this is a solid hard-tier challenge that demands a real multi-step exploit chain.
